requirements.summary: Remove the search/filter input and all associated filtering logic from the public-facing Dashboard page. The Dashboard should display all active and resolved incidents directly without any filtering controls.
acceptanceCriteria: The Dashboard page contains no search input or filter controls
No search/filter state variables exist in the Dashboard component
All active incidents are displayed without any keyword filtering applied
All resolved incidents are displayed without any keyword filtering applied
The 'no results' message tied to the search feature is removed
Removing the search feature does not introduce any new console errors or rendering issues

file_operations.description: Remove all search/filter UI controls (input field, filter dropdowns) and associated state variables (search query, status filter, severity filter) from the Dashboard component. Delete all keyword-matching and filtering logic that filters incidents by title, affected system/service, or description. Ensure both the active incidents section and resolved incidents section render their full lists unconditionally by directly mapping over the unfiltered incident arrays. Remove any conditional rendering tied to empty filter results (e.g., 'no results' messages specific to the search feature). Verify that the component still correctly splits incidents into active and resolved groups based on their status field.
